title: Data & Statistics
objective: Students will explore data & statistics concepts including Comparing measurements, Pictograms and tally charts (age 11+), Line graphs (age 10+) and more.

steps.content: Let's explore Comparing measurements! Have you ever wondered about this?
Describe, interpret, and compare distributions of a single variable using appropriate measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and spread (range), including the effect of outliers
Time to try this out: Calculate mean, median, and mode for a data set and explain when each is most appropriate
If you had the test scores of a whole class, could they find the mean, median, and range, and explain what each tells you about how the class performed?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Comparing measurements: Calculate mean, median, and mode for a data set and explain when each is most appropriate Find the range of a data set and explain how an outlier affects the mean versus the median
Let's explore Pictograms and tally charts (age 11+)! Have you ever wondered about this?

Construct and interpret frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, pictograms, and vertical line charts for both categorical and grouped numerical data, choosing appropriate representations for the data type
Time to try this out: Construct a grouped frequency table from raw continuous data, choosing appropriate class intervals
When you collects data for a project, can they choose the right type of chart to display it — and draw it accurately with correct labels and a suitable scale?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Pictograms and tally charts (age 11+): Construct a grouped frequency table from raw continuous data, choosing appropriate class intervals Draw a pie chart by calculating the angle for each category
Let's discover the amazing world of Line graphs (age 10+)!
Interpret and construct pie charts and line graphs; use these to solve problems
Time to try this out: Read values from a line graph showing temperature over a day and identify trends
Can you read a pie chart — for example, one showing how a family spends its weekly budget — and use it to answer questions and solve problems?

Great job! Today we learned about Line graphs (age 10+). Read values from a line graph showing temperature over a day and identify trends Construct a pie chart from given data by calculating sector angles
Have you ever thought about Calculating the Mean? Let's find out together!
Calculate and interpret the mean as an average of a data set
Let me show you something cool. Calculate the mean of five test scores: 72, 85, 90, 68, 95
If the family scored 6, 8, 7, 5, and 9 on a board game over five rounds, can you work out the mean (average) score?
Great job! Today we learned about Calculating the Mean. Calculate the mean of five test scores: 72, 85, 90, 68, 95 Explain what the mean represents and how it differs from individual data values
Let's explore Understanding fractions! Have you ever wondered about this?

Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8); use operations on fractions to solve problems involving data in line plots (e.g. redistribute total equally)
Time to try this out: Create a line plot showing lengths measured to the nearest 1/8 inch

If you measured the lengths of several objects to the nearest quarter or eighth of an inch and plotted them on a number line, could they use those measurements to work out totals or share amounts equally?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Understanding fractions. Create a line plot showing lengths measured to the nearest 1/8 inch Use the line plot to find the total of all measurements by adding fractions
